title: Ownership, Borrowing, Aliasing, and API Contracts · Object-Lifetime Lens: Transfer On Success
summary: A media index passes buffers through parsers, caches, and callbacks where no component knows who must release or may mutate them. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem transfer on success increment in ownership-annotated buffer API. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.
Classify each pointer as unique owner, shared owner, mutable borrow, immutable borrow, weak observation, or non-owning handle.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Transfer On Success · Theory Transfer On Success
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 2435. Produce bounded C evidence to track owner, borrow, mutation, and release state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.

steps.title: Build Transfer On Success evidence · Theory Transfer On Success
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-transfer-on-success, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-transfer-on-success-iwoxil */" for changed case 1435. It must track owner, borrow, mutation, and release state, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
